original_question:
A rectangular grazing area is to be fenced off on three sides using part of a 100 meter rock wall as the fourth side. Fence posts are to be placed every 12 meters along the fence including the two posts where the fence meets the rock wall. Calculate the fewest number of posts required to fence an area 36 m by 60 m.

Present the answer in LaTex format: \boxed{Your answer}

similar_questions.question: A rectangular grazing area is to be fenced off on three sides using part of a 80 meter rock wall as the fourth side. Fence posts are to be placed every 10 meters along the fence including the two posts where the fence meets the rock wall. Calculate the fewest number of posts required to fence an area 24 m by 40 m.

A rectangular grazing area is to be fenced off on three sides using part of a 90 meter rock wall as the fourth side. Fence posts are to be placed every 15 meters along the fence including the two posts where the fence meets the rock wall. Calculate the fewest number of posts required to fence an area 30 m by 45 m.

A rectangular grazing area is to be fenced off on three sides using part of a 70 meter rock wall as the fourth side. Fence posts are to be placed every 8 meters along the fence including the two posts where the fence meets the rock wall. Calculate the fewest number of posts required to fence an area 18 m by 54 m.

A rectangular grazing area is to be fenced off on three sides using part of a 75 meter rock wall as the fourth side. Fence posts are to be placed every 5 meters along the fence including the two posts where the fence meets the rock wall. Calculate the fewest number of posts required to fence an area 15 m by 60 m.

A rectangular grazing area is to be fenced off on three sides using part of a 85 meter rock wall as the fourth side. Fence posts are to be placed every 20 meters along the fence including the two posts where the fence meets the rock wall. Calculate the fewest number of posts required to fence an area 20 m by 50 m.
